summary: Pristine soundboard recording with a clean mix and strong performance – highlights include Shakedown Street, Estimated Prophet/Eyes of the World and a soaring Scarlet.
review: The May 23‑1982 Greek Theatre show is captured on a well‑mixed soundboard tape that most listeners describe as clear, fat‑bass and exceptionally balanced, despite occasional over‑saturation in the loudest moments. Musically the band delivers a solid first set (Shakedown Street, Red Rooster) and a tight second set with standout jams on Ship of Fools → Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World, plus an energetic Casey Jones closer and a memorable Scarlet encore.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise both the performance and the high‑quality soundboard recording; only a few note minor saturation or tape‑flipping issues, yielding an overall strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced mix with excellent bass response and clean highs; some reviewers note occasional over‑saturation on very loud sections (motorcycle revs) and a few mention tape‑flip constraints for tapers.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is praised for its energy and solid song choices; second set flows tightly through a sequence of strong jams, ending with an uplifting encore that leaves the crowd buzzing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Relaxed vocal on Casey Jones; overall solid guitar work noted indirectly.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mix brings Weir forward in the front‑of‑house sound, especially during Scarlet.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s vocals highlighted on Red Rooster and his Harley was mic'd up, adding a unique texture.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart is noted for interacting with the biker onstage; overall drum presence benefits from the clean Meyer system.
